title: alchemilla vulgaris subsp. heteropoda
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. heteropoda, commonly known as the lady's mantle or bear's foot, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Rosaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The plant has been traditionally used to treat digestive problems, menstrual disorders, and skin conditions, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and alleviating symptoms of menopause. Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. heteropoda can be consumed as a tea, used as a tincture, or applied topically in the form of a cream or salve, making it a versatile and accessible herbal remedy.
